provisions.detail: 5-year initial term from Effective Date. If FA expires without successor and franchisee continues operating, franchisor may treat as either: (i) expired with franchisee operating in violation, or (ii) month-to-month Interim Period terminable by either party on 30 days notice.
Franchisee obligated to operate and pay royalties for the FULL TERM. Must 'continuously exert best efforts to promote and enhance the business.'
One 5-year successor term only. Must sign then-current agreement which may differ materially. Must update Equipment to new-franchisee standards. $2,900 renewal fee. Must sign release.
Royalty 6% (minimum $800/month after month 12), Technology Fee $100/month, all other fees per Item 6. Payment via ACH/EFT. CPI adjustment for fixed fees.

Upon termination, franchisee owes 'the balance of Royalties from the date of termination until the scheduled expiration date of the Franchise Agreement.' This means full royalties for remainder of term even after ceasing operations.
Must submit approved site within 60 days or FA may terminate (no refund of IFF). Cannot execute lease without franchisor approval of terms. Lease must include Lease Addendum.

Must complete within 90 days of Effective Date: secure financing, obtain permits, construct bakery, purchase Equipment, complete training, purchase inventory, obtain insurance. Failure to open within 90 days is material breach.

Must complete Initial Training Program and Pre-Opening Training to franchisor's satisfaction before opening. Additional training may be required at any time at franchisee's expense ($250/day + travel). Annual conference mandatory.
Must comply with all System Standards in Confidential Operations Manual. Modifications may require additional capital investment and/or higher operating expenses.
Extensive operational requirements including approved products only, approved suppliers, minimum inventory, pricing compliance, record-keeping, insurance, maintenance.
3 days for health/safety; 10 days for monetary; 20 days for standards/operations.

15 categories including: 3+ NSF checks/year, failure to open on time, abandonment, unapproved transfer, felony, failure to maintain insurance, repeated defaults (even if cured), bankruptcy, 3+ missed reports in 12 months.
Even if each individual default is cured, repeated defaults constitute non-curable grounds for termination.
Any transfer of FA, business, profits/losses/capital appreciation rights, substantially all assets, or any ownership interest requires prior written approval. Transfer without approval = breach.

17 conditions including: not in default, all amounts paid, transferee qualifies, not competitor, training complete, sign current FA (may differ materially), sign release, guaranty, ROFR offered, upgrade/remodel with deposit, post-transfer noncompete, reimburse all transfer costs including broker fees.
Franchisor may match any offer for the business or ownership interest within 30 days.
No diverting business; no ownership interest in or services for competitive business anywhere.
2 years, 50-mile radius from (i) franchisee's business, (ii) territory, (iii) ALL other Cookie Advantage businesses. Tolling provision extends restricted period by duration of any violation.
All direct and indirect owners must sign personal guaranty. Spouses of franchise owners must also sign. Joint and several liability. Unlimited guaranty — covers all obligations of the entity.
Spouse must sign guaranty making them liable for financial obligations even without ownership interest. Both marital and personal assets, including house, at risk.
Mediation first (non-binding, Bixby OK, 60 days). Then binding arbitration (AAA or franchisor-selected, Bixby OK). Compulsory counterclaims or forever barred.
All litigation and arbitration in Bixby, Oklahoma. Oklahoma law applies.
Jury trial waiver. Punitive/exemplary damages waiver. 1-year statute of limitations.

Required: comprehensive public/product/motor vehicle liability, general casualty (replacement value), workers comp, business interruption (min 6 months), employment practices liability, data theft, umbrella ($1M/$3M). Insurer must be A.M. Best 'A' or better. Must name franchisor as additional insured. Waiver of subrogation required.
Franchisee must indemnify and reimburse franchisor for any expenses or losses related in any way to the Cookie Advantage Business or Franchise. Broad scope — 'related in any way.'

Franchisor owns ALL data generated by the Computer System including customer lists. Franchisor has independent remote access to all electronic data. No contractual limitation on franchisor's right to use this data, including posting financial information on intranet.

Pay all outstanding amounts including accelerated royalties through scheduled expiration. Complete de-identification. Return Confidential Information. Deliver entire customer file. Transfer/forward phone numbers. Refund customers entitled to refunds. Comply with noncompete.
Franchisor may purchase the business and premises within 30 days after termination.
No force majeure clause identified in the Franchise Agreement.
If FA expires without successor and franchisee continues, franchisor may elect month-to-month continuation — but franchisee's right to successor term is null and void during this period.
Royalties owed through remainder of originally scheduled term, even after termination. This is an unusually aggressive provision.
Franchisor exclusively reserves internet as channel of distribution. Franchisee may not market on internet or conduct e-commerce.
Post-term restricted period extended by duration of any violation — effectively indefinite if franchisee is found in violation.

system_protection_agreement.injunctive_relief: Available without bond (or $1,000 bond if court requires)

key_provisions: Franchisor right to assignment of lease on termination/expiration
Franchisor right to sublease to another franchisee without landlord consent
Landlord must copy default notices to franchisor; franchisor has cure rights (tenant cure period + 10 days)
No lease termination or material amendment without franchisor written consent
Franchisor right to enter premises on termination to remove signs and de-identify
Landlord waives lien rights on assets purchased by franchise assignee
